title: Contingency Plan — Backup Job Success for ePHI Databases

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses §164.308(a)(7) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(7) (Contingency plan) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.8: Contingency Plan — Backup Job Success for ePHI Databases.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.8' running on sourcetype commvault:job and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=backup` `sourcetype=commvault:job` or `rubrik:events`, SQL `msdb.dbo.backupset` via DBX, Epic Clarity backup logs if forwarded
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
spl:
index=backup sourcetype="commvault:job" earliest=-48h (database="CLARITY" OR database="CABOODLE" OR app="Epic*")
| eval success=if(match(lower(status),"success|completed"),1,0)
| stats sum(success) as ok_jobs count as total_jobs latest(_time) as last_job by database, client_name
| eval health=if(ok_jobs=total_jobs,"OK","FAIL")
| where health!="OK"
description: Monitors backup completion for Clarity/Caboodle and clinical data stores so availability safeguards in the contingency plan are continuously evidenced.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes backup and sourcetypes commvault:job, commvault:job`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Tag databases containing ePHI in backup catalog; (2) page DBAs on failure; (3) quarterly restore test outcomes ingested as separate sourcetype for correlation. Indexes required: `index=backup`.
visualization: Single value (failed jobs), Table (DB, last success), Timeline (job duration anomalies).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=backup` `sourcetype=commvault:job` or `rubrik:events`, SQL `msdb.dbo.backupset` via DBX, Epic Clarity backup logs if forwarded.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag databases containing ePHI in backup catalog; (2) page DBAs on failure; (3) quarterly restore test outcomes ingested as separate sourcetype for correlation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=backup sourcetype="commvault:job" earliest=-48h (database="CLARITY" OR database="CABOODLE" OR app="Epic*")
| eval success=if(match(lower(status),"success|completed"),1,0)
| stats sum(success) as ok_jobs count as total_jobs latest(_time) as last_job by database, client_name
| eval health=if(ok_jobs=total_jobs,"OK","FAIL")
| where health!="OK"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Contingency Plan — Backup Job Success for ePHI Databases (§164.308(a)(7))** — Monitors backup completion for Clarity/Caboodle and clinical data stores so availability safeguards in the contingency plan are continuously evidenced.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=backup` `sourcetype=commvault:job` or `rubrik:events`, SQL `msdb.dbo.backupset` via DBX, Epic Clarity backup logs if forwarded. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: backup; **sourcetype**: commvault:job. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=backup, sourcetype="commvault:job", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **success** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by database, client_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **health** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where health!="OK"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (failed jobs), Table (DB, last success), Timeline (job duration anomalies).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for monitors backup completion for clarity/caboodle and clinical data stores so availability safeguards in the contingency plan are continuously evidenced. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
